Details
This is the gamma (0.4) publication of the UK digital identity and attributes
trust framework. It is the first statutory trust framework (‘the DVS trust
framework') under section 28 of the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (‘the
Act')

sections.heading: This statutory publication supersedes the June 2025 pre-statutory
sections.text:
This statutory publication supersedes the June 2025 pre-statutory
publication, but certifications against pre-statutory publications will remain
valid under the conditions described in Section 0.
The June 2025 final publication, in turn, superseded the November 2024
pre-release that was shared for business readiness purposes.
The UK Accreditation Service has recognised this publication, and approved
conformity assessment bodies (CABs) (https://www.gov.uk/guidance/list-of-
approved-conformity-assessment-bodies) could begin certifying services against
its requirements from 1 July 2025.
Minimal changes were made to the content of the final publication document
after the pre-release, with only small necessary corrections (outlined in
Section 0) as well as alterations to the enforcement and expiry dates in the
‘Version and certification validity notes' (also Section 0).
Further information on how digital verification services can get certified
(https://www.gov.uk/guidance/join-the-register-of-digital-identity-and-attribute-
services) can be found on the Office for Digital Identities and Attributes
(https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/office-for-digital-identities-and-
attributes) website.
Published 25 November 2024
